page: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton3 processor family. It features 64 dedicated physical ARMv8 cores based on the Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The r7g.16xlarge achieves top-tier results in multi-core Geekbench tests and software compilation, alongside high Passmark memory scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 6200.54 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 253.34 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also strong, with Redis handling 101.19 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average range, and small-model LLM text generation is relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth per unit of resource consumption compared to traditional x86 architectures. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that scale across multiple cores. Recommended workloads include large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, software compilation pipelines, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by 64 dedicated physical ARM64 cores on the AWS Graviton3 architecture running at 2.6 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier multi-core capabilities, software compilation speeds, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and Redis performance. However, single-core execution and small-model text generation are less competitive. Qualitatively, the ARM64 architecture offers high memory density and processing efficiency for multi-threaded applications. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large databases, in-memory caching, and concurrent software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth, built for high-throughput database and memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated cores, 512 GB RAM, and 30 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores (AWS Graviton3 Neoverse-V1) at 2.6 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and software compilation benchmarks
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and concurrent workloads
